characters.name: Atticus Fetch
characters.description: Atticus "Ollie" Fetchis a rock musician thatHank Moodywas writing a musical for. He is addicted to cocaine. Atticus is the perfect example of a man who got too much too soon. He is a mentally stunted, naive manchild that is used to getting whatever he wants, whenever he wants it and gets furious if he doesn't get it right away. When he madeCharliehis manager, he would often take his anger out on him. Much likeLew AshbyandRick Springfield, Atticus is a chauvinist that sees women as his own personal playthings. Atticus is also a desperate coke addict. He gets extremely agitated from cocaine withdrawal and takes it out on others, almost breaking his friendTony's Martin acoustic and would have unlessFaithdidn't stop him. Atticus is the perfect example of a man who got too much too soon. He is a mentally stunted, naive manchild that is used to getting whatever he wants, whenever he wants it and gets furious if he doesn't get it right away. When he madeCharliehis manager, he would often take his anger out on him. Much likeLew AshbyandRick Springfield, Atticus is a chauvinist that sees women as his own personal playthings. Atticus is also a desperate coke addict. He gets extremely agitated from cocaine withdrawal and takes it out on others, almost breaking his friendTony's Martin acoustic and would have unlessFaithdidn't stop him. Despite his flaws, Atticus is ironically enough a romantic. He believes that love is the ultimate force that keeps the world going and is willing to let Hank pursue his dreams withKareninstead of joining him on tour.

characters.name: Bill Lewis
characters.description: Bill Lewisis the fiance ofHank's estranged loverKaren Van der Beek, and owner of the magazineHell-A, which hired Hank in the first season to write a blog. His personality is very uptight and overly careful, which makes him the polar opposite of Hank Moody. He is usually very interested in his work, to the point that he doesn't really pay much attention to his daughterMia, and thinks of her as a perfect child who can do no wrong. It is implied through certain dialogue that Bill may be impotent as well. Furthermore, he was the fiance of Karen, but she left him after the wedding.

characters.name: Charlie Runkle
characters.description: Used Car Salesman Stuart Runkle(son) Charles Wolfgang "Charlie" Runkleis a Main character in all 7 seasons ofCalifornication. He is a former Hollywood talent agent, most notably ofHank Moody.

characters.name: Charlie Runkle
characters.description: Used Car Salesman Stuart Runkle(son) Charles Wolfgang "Charlie" Runkleis a Main character in all 7 seasons ofCalifornication. He is a former Hollywood talent agent, most notably ofHank Moody.

characters.name: Dani California
characters.description: Dani Californiais a former assistant ofCharlie Runkle, appearing in the first and second seasons ofCalifornication.

characters.name: Eddie Nero
characters.description: Eddie Nerois a Recurring character in theFourth,Fifth,SixthandSeventhseasons ofCalifornication. He is a Academy Award winning Hollywood actor.

characters.name: Eddie Nero
characters.description: Eddie Nerois a Recurring character in theFourth,Fifth,SixthandSeventhseasons ofCalifornication. He is a Academy Award winning Hollywood actor.

characters.name: Hank Moody
characters.description: "I'm disgusted with my life and myself, but I'm not unhappy about that." - Hank Moody Henry James "Hank" Moodyis the main character ofCalifornication, with the show revolving around him trying to fix his relationship with his daughterRebeccaand his on and off again girlfriendKaren, while also battling with alcoholism, drug abuse and sex addiction. He is a famed novelist and a freelance writer, and the star client and best friend ofCharlie Runkle. His literary works includeSouth of Heaven, Seasons in the AbyssandGod Hates Us All, the latter of which was adapted into a move calledACrazy Little Thing Called Love, which Hank admittedly hates for its vast departure from his original book. Although in his early forties, Hank seems to think of himself as, and act like, a man in his twenties. The profits from his books have enabled him to live a relatively comfortable and carefree lifestyle, one of the reasons for his arrested development in certain areas. The Love he has for books is out of this so called I’m just a writer. He was on Hell-A magazine and loves to read. Despite this, Hank still loves and cares deeply for his teenage daughter Becca, as well as his on-again off-again love interestKaren(Becca's mother). Still, Hank tends to find himself in trouble more often than not, with his daughter in school, and an abundance of time on his hands. One of Hank's major character flaws (and source of drama on the show), is his tendency to effortlessly attract women to him, bringing with them an assortment of trouble that usually often damages his family life. Being a successful writer, many women feel that they are already connected to Hank through his works, making it that much easier for Hank to respond to their flirtations. With Karen often out of town for her work, Hank tends to give in to these women more often than not, although sometimes he will try to restrain himself. A major effect of his promiscuity is that his daughter is often around to witness it, thus making things between Becca and Hank even more difficult. Despite this, Hank is still motivated by and dedicated to (in a way) his relationship with Karen. Another major source of trouble for Hank is hanging out with his friends. Whether it's music producerLew Ashbyand a drug-fueled all night sex party, or his best friend/agentCharlie Runkle; trouble is never far from the man. Hank and Charlie always seem to have a great time together, yet at the end of the night/morning, they know that it's been a wild ride. Examples include double-teaming a female boxing instructor, stealing from a bookstore (his own work no less), and getting tattoos while not remembering the previous night. Hank is an esteemed but erratic writer who frequently becomes embroiled in bizarre, and in some cases, scandalous situations. He has been described as the ultimate womanizer: he's charming, witty, handsome and, according to his former attorney Abby, "pretty good at math". It can be assumed that Hank suffers from depressio

characters.name: Karen van der Beek
characters.description: (home designer) Karen van der Beekis a main character in the whole series ofCalifornication. the former girlfriend ofHank Moody. They had one daughter, while together, namedBecca. Karen left her fiance,Billat the altar to return to Hank, however they parted again, shortly after.

characters.name: Karen van der Beek
characters.description: (home designer) Karen van der Beekis a main character in the whole series ofCalifornication. the former girlfriend ofHank Moody. They had one daughter, while together, namedBecca. Karen left her fiance,Billat the altar to return to Hank, however they parted again, shortly after.

characters.name: Levon
characters.description: LevonisHank Moody's illegitimate son withJulia, whom Hank had fathered before meetingKaren. Levon arrives atCharlie Runkle's home, having sent a dozen emails to Charlie over setting up an interview with Hank Moody.

characters.name: Lew Ashby
characters.description: "Life is just too fucking boring not to try, OK?" - Lew Ashby Lew Ashbyis a music producer featured inseason 2. Lew makes his first appearance as the host of wild, recurring parties at his huge mansion in Laurel Canyon. Not really caring who shows and who doesn't, he lets pretty much anyone, Hank,Karen,CharlieandMarcyincluded, enter his home during these events. As Hank and Karen were about to get comfortable in one of his bedrooms, Hank needed to visit the bathroom. He got lost and ended up performing the cunnilingus on one of Lew's loose arrangements, earning the title of "Mouth Rapist" much to his dismay. Lew is very much like Hank, only a million times worse in every way. Where Hank is trying to numb his pain, Lew is a genuine chauvinist with hedonistic tendencies. He seems to think of most women as mere objects of lust, excluding the love of his lifeJanie Jonesand Karen, with whom he developed an unlikely friendship the two referred to as a "truce". Lew seems to lack any form of impulse control whatsoever and often does things like having sex with a minor or hosting drug-fueled parties out of a momentary whim. His lifestyle is, in fact, so self-destructive that even Hank has difficulties with understanding him at times. Lew is also addicted to cocaine to the point where it would claim his life, mistakenly snorting heroin thinking it was cocaine. Despite his flaws, Lew means well most of the time and is a loyal friend. Suffering from loneliness and a likely case of depression, he fears the idea of losing Hank from his life, referring to him as his"only friend". The show hints that Lew has produced some of the most successful Rock albums of the 80's. This makes him wealthy and well connected to the entertainment circles of Hollywood. This also gives him the opportunity to act very generously if he so pleases. For example he gifted Hank Kurt Cobain's old guitar, got him on a sex date withChloe Metz, a successful TV chef and invited Karen to a private concert at the Hollywood Bowl.

characters.name: Marcy Runkle
characters.description: Stuart Runkle (son) Marcy Runkleis a major character in thefirst,second,third,fourth,fifth,sixthandseventhseasons ofCalifornication. She is the wife ofCharlie Runkle, having divorced him by the end of the third season, but finally remarrying him by the end of the sixth season, as well as being the mother of their sonStuart Runkle. She is the best friend ofKaren, and is seen as an aunt to her and Hanks daughterBecca. She is the ex-wife ofStu Beggs, having divorced him by the beginning of season six. In the three year gap between season four and five, Marcy had both given birth to her and Charlie's son Stuart, as well as marryingStu Beggs. Marcy has been experiencing a "sexual renaissance" while married to Stu.

Marcy has been experiencing a "sexual renaissance" while married to Stu.

characters.name: Mia Lewis
characters.description: Mia Lewis (pen name Mia Cross)is the teenage daughter ofBill Lewis, and had sex withHank Moodyin thePilot. Hank subsequently overcame his writer's block after this, and wrote the treatment for a novel; which Mia then stole, adapted, and published as her own work entitledFucking and Punching. For her part, Mia seems to enjoy holding a bit of power over Hank, namely the fact that she can let Karen know about the one-night stand at any time. Mia is generally very aggressive sexually, and is used to getting what she wants. Despite this, she is still a minor (16 in the pilot), and knows that targeting older men is wrong.

characters.name: Rebecca Moody
characters.description: Grace(Dream) Rebecca "Becca" Moodyis the gothic teenage daughter ofHank MoodyandKaren van der Beek. She is very intelligent and musically inclined for her age, as well as precocious. Between season 4 and season 5, Becca had met and began a relationship withTyler Stevens Becca is savvy enough to call out her father for his indiscretions. Becca generally acts as Hank's conscience throughout the run of the show, and she is arguably the most affected by his reckless behavior. On the whole, her character adds a little dark, heart-breaking side to the seemingly all-fun fuck-around of the adults and gives the show much greater depth.

characters.name: Richard Bates
characters.description: Richard Batesis a recurring character in the third and fifth seasons ofCalifornication, with a minor appearance in the sixth season. During the 3 year gap between seasons 4 and 5, Richard Bates would again enter into a relationship with Karen, eventually marrying her. When Hank returns to LA to escape his ex girlfriend Carrie, he visits Bates and Karen at their home.

characters.name: Samurai Apocalypse
characters.description: Samurai Apocalypseis a rapper and businessman. He appeares in the fifth season. He hired Hank to help write his tv Santa Monica cop. He is often nicknamed Sam. He is seen with his crew at most times whether at his house or when out. He amusingly has a white butler who he calls Alfred (although this is not his actual name, and likely calls him this as a reference to Alfred Pennyworth, Batman's butler). Sam hired Hank to write the movie of Santa Monica Cop. Which is like Beverly Hills cop but based in Santa Monica.

characters.name: Stu Beggs
characters.description: Stewart "Stu" Beggsis a recurring character in thefourth,fifth,sixthandseventhseasons ofCalifornication. He is a big Hollywood producer known by many women for his large penis. Between the fourth and fifth seasons he is married toMarcy Runkle, only for her to divorce him between season five and six. He is first introduced inepisode oneof season 4, while at the meeting to discuss the movieFucking and Punching. Stew is an obvious caricature portrait based on prolific producer Shep Gordon, featured in the documentary, “Supermensche: The Legend of Shep Gordon”.

characters.name: Stu Beggs
characters.description: Stewart "Stu" Beggsis a recurring character in thefourth,fifth,sixthandseventhseasons ofCalifornication. characters.name: Stuart Runkle
characters.description: Stuart Runkleis the son ofCharlieandMarcy Runkle. He was named after his ex-step fatherStu Beggs. Marcy discovered she was pregnant with Stuart while datingStu Beggs. At first, she told Stu the baby was his, but revealed the truth in...And Justice for All, revealing that Charlie was the actual father. Stu was born two and a half years before the beginning of Season 5, and arrived with Charlie at the airport to pick upHank Moody. Hank sees Stuart as an honorary nephew, already seeing Charlie as his brother. Stu is still incapable of speaking, even at two and a half years old, with Marcy and Stu saying it's completely normal, which Hank disagrees with.

characters.name: Tyler Stevens
characters.description: Tyler Stevensis    the boyfriend ofRebecca moody. He is portrayed byScott Michael Foster Before the events of season five, Tyler met and began a relationship with Becca. Secretly, he'd only met Becca in order to meetHank Moody, her dad, and make his way into professional screenwriter, but he claims he truly did fall in love with Becca
